nativeRange.habitat: Coral and rocky reefs with abundant hiding spaces

diet.notes: Generally accepts meaty foods including finely chopped fresh or frozen marine fish, squid, mysis shrimp, and vitamin-enriched brine shrimp. Has been observed consuming small ornamental shrimp and bristleworms in the tank.

goodWith: moderately aggressive fish of similar size or larger

imageCandidates.notes: Pseudochromis fridmani (Orchid Dottyback), Cangadoba, 5472x3648. Tack-sharp, brilliant magenta full-body specimen; the most striking image in the genus pool.
Pseudochromis aldabraensis (Neon/Orange Dottyback), Haplochromis, 1592x896. Sharp orange body with electric-blue lined markings, whole fish in frame.
Pseudochromis moorei, Jim Greenfield, 1873x1249. Sharp vivid orange body with fine dot rows and blue fin edges.
Pseudochromis erdmanni, iNaturalist user craigj, 1470x980. Sharp orange head with black bars over a purple-grey body.
Pseudochromis marshallensis, Wayne and Pam Osborn, 2048x940. Sharp; grey body with orange spot rows, red dorsal and yellow tail.
Pseudochromis ammeri (Raja Dottyback), Rickard Zerpe, 2604x1953. Sharp; white face, black lateral stripe and bright yellow belly stripe.
Pseudochromis springeri (Springer's Dottyback), PilarMeca, 3072x2048. Sharp; dark blue-black body with neon-blue lines, full fish.
Pseudochromis steenei (Lyretail Dottyback), Rickard Zerpe, 4386x3290. Sharp; orange head, grey body, yellow lyretail.
Pseudochromis caudalis, AravindManoj, 5623x3828. Sharp studio shot on black; minor corner signature watermark (not over subject).

summary: The Red Spotted Dottyback (Pseudochromis sp.) is a pale-bodied Indian Ocean reef fish patterned with lined red spots. Generally considered suitable for intermediate keepers, it tends to be semi-aggressive and is best kept singly with moderately sized tankmates.
careNotes: The Red Spotted Dottyback is an undescribed Pseudochromis species currently sold in the trade under the designation Pseudochromis sp. Its pale body and fins are decorated with red spots arranged in lined patterns. It is generally considered semi-aggressive and has been known to challenge tankmates; it is best paired with moderately aggressive fish larger than itself. A well-sealed lid is advisable, as dottybacks have been reported to jump. Plenty of rockwork and hiding spaces are important for this species to feel secure. It tends to be kept singly, as conspecific aggression with other dottybacks is commonly observed. It may prey on small ornamental shrimp and bristleworms. The species is generally considered reef-safe with corals and most invertebrates larger than small shrimp.

additional.name: FishBase
additional.notes: Designated secondary (FishBase ID 7461) resolves to Pictichromis porphyrea (Magenta Dottyback), not this species. Used for family/order taxonomy (Pseudochromidae, Ovalentaria) and genus-level habitat and parameter cross-reference only. No species-specific page exists for Pseudochromis sp. (Red Spotted Dottyback) as it is undescribed.

additional.name: LiveAquaria (Bicolor Dottyback cross-ref)
additional.notes: Cross-checked water parameters and tank-size conventions against a closely related Pseudochromis species (P. paccagnellae). Parameters 72-78°F, pH 8.1-8.4, SG 1.020-1.025, dKH 8-12 are consistent across the genus.
